name: Appalachian Community
description_short: Member-owned federal credit union serving 90+ years across Tennessee, Virginia, and Kentucky with auto loans, home financing, and deposit products.
description_long:
Appalachian Community Federal Credit Union (ACFCU) was originally established in 1932 by employees of the Mead Paper Mill (now Domtar) in Kingsport, Tennessee. Over nine decades, it has evolved from a single-employer credit union into a regional financial cooperative serving multiple counties across three states. The institution operates as a not-for-profit, member-owned organization governed by its borrowers and depositors rather than external shareholders.

ACFCU offers a comprehensive suite of consumer financial products including auto loans (as low as 5.55% APR), auto refinancing with promotional terms (no payments for 60 days, no loan fees, same-day pre-approvals), home loans with up to 100% financing available, motorcycle loans, boat and RV loans, personal loans, toy loans, money market accounts (up to 0.80% APY), and certificates of deposit (up to 3.50% APY). Members can access online account management through their myACFCU portal, make loan payments online, and apply for new credit digitally.

The credit union differentiates itself through its "Appy Way" member-centric messaging, emphasis on hometown approvals for mortgages, and regional community focus across nine counties in Tennessee, Virginia, and Kentucky plus specific cities (Bristol, TN/VA and Norton, VA). Recent recognition includes a 2026 MemberXP Best of the Best Award for delivering exceptional member experiences. The organization invests in financial education and insurance products for members, and has recently expanded leadership with new branch managers.

As a credit union, ACFCU operates under NCUA federal insurance rather than FDIC coverage, and membership eligibility is restricted to those who live, work, worship, or attend school in specific geographic service areas or designated entities. While rates are competitive and service is praised by members, the geographic membership restriction may limit accessibility for consumers outside the Appalachian region.
services: Auto loans and auto refinancing with rates from 5.55% APR
Home loans with up to 100% financing for purchase and refinance
Boat and RV loans (7.00% APR minimum)
Toy loans for recreational vehicle financing
Money market accounts with rates up to 0.80% APY
Certificates of deposit with rates up to 3.50% APY
myACFCU online account management and digital banking portal
Financial education resources and member support
Insurance and financial planning advisory services
pros: Auto refinance rates as low as 5.55% APR with promotional incentives (60 days no payment, no fees, same-day pre-approvals)
Home loans available with up to 100% financing—no down payment required
Certificates of deposit offering competitive yields up to 3.50% APY
Nine decades of operational history and regional stability since 1932
Online loan application and approval with quick processing times
Diverse product range including toy loans, motorcycle loans, and RV financing
Recognized with 2026 MemberXP Best of the Best Award for member experience
cons: Geographic membership restriction limits access to specific counties in Tennessee, Virginia, and Kentucky only
Personal loan rates start at 9.75% APR, which is higher than some competitive alternatives
Money market and savings rates are modest (0.80% APY maximum) compared to high-yield online banks
Membership eligibility requires verification of residency, employment, or other qualifying status in service area
Limited visibility into loan approval criteria, processing timelines, or detailed fee structures from website content alone
best_for: Residents of the Appalachian region (Tennessee, Virginia, Kentucky service areas) seeking competitive auto and mortgage financing
Homebuyers in the service area who want local relationship lending and high loan-to-value mortgage options
Members prioritizing community-based credit union membership over national banks
Savers looking for certificate of deposit products with moderate yields and NCUA protection
diagnosis: Best for residents, employees, and students within ACFCU's nine-county service area across Tennessee, Virginia, and Kentucky seeking relationship-based lending with competitive rates. Primary caveat is strict geographic membership eligibility—those outside the service area cannot join, making this unsuitable for national consumers or those relocating outside the region.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
meta_description: Appalachian Community — NCUA-insured credit union in Kingsport, TN. Member-owned, not-for-profit. Loans, savings, and member services.
